mission.title: crossplane: Dangling CRD can block provider re-installation
mission.description: Dangling CRD can block provider re-installation. This issue affects 43+ users.

steps.title: Identify crossplane troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your crossplane deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane -n crossplane --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What happened?

We are running into a situation where a provider CRD which is stuck in deletion can stop (but not block) the re-installation of the same provider.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Dangling CRD can block provider re-installation
steps.description:
>I believe we've seen this in our CI cluster a few times, and I believe clearing the ownerReferences on the CRDs is a workaround/temporary fix.

Yes this works as a workaround and allows for the re-installation of the provider. It leads to something pretty interesting:
It unblocks the re-creation
```yaml
helm install crossplane \
--namespace crossplane-system \
--create-namespace crossplane-stable/crossplane \
--version 1.18.2

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: pkg.crossplane.io/v1
kind: Provider
metadata:
  name: provider-dummy
spec:
    package: xpkg.upbound.io/upbound/provider-dummy:v0.3.0
EOF

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: iam.dummy.upbound.io/v1alpha1
kind: Robot
metadata:
  name: example
spec:
  forProvider:
    color: yellow
EOF
```

steps.title: Upgrade crossplane to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade crossplane:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade crossplane crossplane/crossplane --namespace crossplane
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane
helm list -n crossplane
```

steps.title: Confirm Dangling CRD can block provider re-installation is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane -n crossplane --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
>I believe we've seen this in our CI cluster a few times, and I believe clearing the ownerReferences on the CRDs is a workaround/temporary fix.

Yes this works as a workaround and allows for the re-installation of the provider.
codeSnippets:
helm install crossplane \
--namespace crossplane-system \
--create-namespace crossplane-stable/crossplane \
--version 1.18.2

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: pkg.crossplane.io/v1
kind: Provider
metadata:
  name: provider-dummy
spec:
    package: xpkg.upbound.io/upbound/provider-dummy:v0.3.0
EOF

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: iam.dummy.upbound.io/v1alpha1
kind: Robot
metadata:
  name: example
spec:
  forProvider:
    color: yellow
EOF

k patch robots.iam.dummy.upbound.io example -p '{"metadata":{"finalizers":["example"]}}' --type=merge

k delete providers provider-dummy

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
